evals.prompt:
Write a book chapter from the sample session findings. Use the non-fiction genre.

Findings directory: $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings

IMPORTANT: synthesize-artifact.sh changes directory internally, so both scripts must write their outputs into the current working directory using absolute paths built from pwd. Run the pipeline as follows:

  OUTDIR=$(pwd)
  $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/synthesize-artifact.sh "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings" non-fiction "$OUTDIR/artifact.json"
  $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/render-artifact.sh "$OUTDIR/artifact.json" book "$OUTDIR/chapter.md"
evals.expected_output: A book chapter written to chapter.md and an intermediate artifact written to artifact.json. The chapter must open with '# Chapter:' title, include a '## Endnotes' section with public source URLs, and must not contain any internal MIF identifiers, URN strings, corpus paths, or finding IDs.

deterministic_checks.description: Chapter Markdown file was produced
Intermediate artifact JSON was produced
Chapter opens with a book-format title
Chapter ends with an Endnotes section
At least one surviving finding's public source URL appears in endnotes
No MIF internal URN identifiers leak into the manuscript
No internal finding IDs appear in the chapter
No harness schema paths appear in the chapter
No corpus paths appear in the chapter
No internal f_<dimension>_<n> finding handles in the manuscript
Intermediate artifact is valid JSON
Artifact carries a newsworthiness field
Artifact has at least 3 sections (one per surviving finding)

expectations: The chapter opens with '# Chapter:' on the first line followed immediately by a genre/audience blockquote on line 3.
The first section body serves as the chapter introduction with no heading; subsequent sections each carry a numbered heading like '### 1.' and '### 2.'.
Every endnote entry resolves to a publicly accessible URL (copier.readthedocs.io or cookiecutter.readthedocs.io) — no internal corpus paths, no urn: scheme URIs.
The chapter prose is written in coherent paragraphs that synthesize research content, not a literal dump of raw finding fields or JSON.

evals.prompt:
Write a book chapter using the fiction genre from the sample session findings.

Findings directory: $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings

IMPORTANT: synthesize-artifact.sh changes directory internally, so both scripts must write their outputs into the current working directory using absolute paths built from pwd. Run the pipeline as follows:

  OUTDIR=$(pwd)
  $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/synthesize-artifact.sh "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings" fiction "$OUTDIR/artifact.json"
  $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/render-artifact.sh "$OUTDIR/artifact.json" book "$OUTDIR/chapter.md"
evals.expected_output: A book chapter written to chapter.md and artifact.json. The artifact's genre field must be 'fiction'. The chapter must include a genre/audience header line and an Endnotes section with public source URLs. The citation-leak gate must hold regardless of genre.

deterministic_checks.description: Chapter file was produced
Intermediate artifact JSON was produced
Artifact genre field is set to fiction
Rendered chapter blockquote shows the fiction genre
Chapter includes an Endnotes section
At least one public source URL appears in endnotes
No MIF internal URNs in the chapter
No internal finding IDs in the chapter
No harness schema paths in the chapter
No internal f_<dimension>_<n> finding handles in the chapter
Artifact JSON is valid
Artifact has at least 3 sections from surviving findings

expectations: The genre blockquote in the rendered chapter reads 'Genre: fiction · audience: general' (or similar), confirming the genre argument was threaded through the synthesizer.
All surviving findings from the sample session appear in the chapter — genre is a rendering hint passed to the synthesizer, not a content filter.
Endnotes cite only public URLs (copier.readthedocs.io, cookiecutter.readthedocs.io) — no internal URN or corpus paths appear regardless of genre.

evals.prompt:
Demonstrate that falsified findings are excluded from the book chapter.

Set up a temporary findings directory containing:
1. The 3 surviving sample findings (copy these):
   $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings/finding-copier.json
   $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings/finding-cookiecutter.json
   $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings/finding-distribution.json
2. A 4th finding with verdict 'falsified'. Write it to the temp dir as finding-falsified.json with this exact JSON content:
{"@context":"https://mif-spec.dev/schema/context.jsonld","@type":"Concept","@id":"urn:mif:concept:harness:falsified-sentinel-0099","title":"FALSIFIED-SENTINEL-XYZ technology claim","content":"FALSIFIED-SENTINEL-XYZ content that must not appear in published output.","summary":"FALSIFIED-SENTINEL-XYZ summary.","namespace":"harness/example-topic","citations":[{"@type":"Citation","title":"FALSIFIED-SENTINEL-XYZ source","url":"https://FALSIFIED-SENTINEL-XYZ.example.com/"}],"extensions":{"harness":{"dimension":"technical","verification":{"verdict":"falsified","verdict_basis":"Disconfirmed by adversarial search."}}}}

Then synthesize and render the chapter.

IMPORTANT: synthesize-artifact.sh changes directory internally, so both scripts must write their outputs into the current working directory using absolute paths built from pwd:

  OUTDIR=$(pwd)
  TMPDIR=$(mktemp -d)
  cp "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings/finding-copier.json" "$TMPDIR/"
  cp "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings/finding-cookiecutter.json" "$TMPDIR/"
  cp "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings/finding-distribution.json" "$TMPDIR/"
  # write finding-falsified.json to $TMPDIR/finding-falsified.json
  $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/synthesize-artifact.sh "$TMPDIR" non-fiction "$OUTDIR/artifact.json"
  $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/render-artifact.sh "$OUTDIR/artifact.json" book "$OUTDIR/chapter.md"
evals.expected_output: A book chapter from exactly 3 surviving findings. The sentinel string FALSIFIED-SENTINEL-XYZ must not appear anywhere in chapter.md or artifact.json. The artifact must have exactly 3 sections and 3 finding_refs. The chapter must contain copier.readthedocs.io as confirmation of real content from surviving findings.

deterministic_checks.description: Chapter was produced
Artifact was produced
Sentinel string from the falsified finding is absent from the chapter
Sentinel string is absent from the synthesized artifact
The falsified finding's source URL does not appear in endnotes
A surviving finding's public URL is present — confirms real content was rendered, not an empty/aborted run
No MIF internal URNs in the chapter
Artifact is valid JSON
Artifact has exactly 3 sections — the falsified finding was excluded, not merely masked
finding_refs has exactly 3 entries — the falsified finding's @id is excluded from the refs list

expectations: The transcript shows the skill created a temp directory containing 4 JSON files (3 surviving sample findings + finding-falsified.json with the FALSIFIED-SENTINEL-XYZ sentinel) before running synthesize-artifact.sh.
The synthesizer's stdout confirms 3 sections and 2 sources, and the rendered chapter contains no trace of FALSIFIED-SENTINEL-XYZ or its source URL FALSIFIED-SENTINEL-XYZ.example.com.
The chapter endnotes cite only public sources from the 3 surviving findings — copier.readthedocs.io and cookiecutter.readthedocs.io.

evals.prompt:
Write a book chapter from the sample session findings using the technical genre. Verify the full structural layout and that sources are correctly deduplicated — two of the three findings share the same copier.readthedocs.io URL, so the endnotes must contain exactly 2 unique source entries.

Findings directory: $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings

IMPORTANT: synthesize-artifact.sh changes directory internally, so both scripts must write their outputs into the current working directory using absolute paths built from pwd. Run the pipeline as follows:

  OUTDIR=$(pwd)
  $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/synthesize-artifact.sh "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings" technical "$OUTDIR/artifact.json"
  $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/render-artifact.sh "$OUTDIR/artifact.json" book "$OUTDIR/chapter.md"
evals.expected_output: A book chapter in chapter.md with full structural layout: '# Chapter:' title, genre blockquote, intro paragraph, '### 1.' and '### 2.' numbered subsections, and '## Endnotes' with exactly 2 deduplicated entries (copier.readthedocs.io and cookiecutter.readthedocs.io). No internal MIF plumbing.

deterministic_checks.description: Chapter file was produced
Chapter title is in book format
First numbered subsection is present
Second numbered subsection is present
Endnotes section is present
First public source URL appears in endnotes
Second public source URL appears in endnotes
No MIF internal URNs in the chapter
No corpus paths in the chapter
Artifact is valid JSON
Artifact carries a newsworthiness field
Sources are deduplicated to exactly 2 unique URLs (copier and cookiecutter share one domain; copier URL appears in 2 findings but deduplicates to 1)

expectations: The chapter has the exact structure: '# Chapter: <title>' on line 1, a blockquote with genre/audience on line 3, the first section body as prose intro (no heading), then '### 1. <heading>' and '### 2. <heading>' subsections.
The Endnotes section contains exactly 2 entries — '[1]' and '[2]' — because the copier.readthedocs.io URL appears in two findings but is deduplicated by the synthesizer's unique_by(.url) pass.
The chapter reads as coherent prose derived from the findings, not raw JSON field values or a template skeleton with placeholder text.
